player_utterance: You carried a Bible to the burial. Did you open it?

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, clicking a pen without noticing, paper cup of cold coffee in his other hand
sitting down on the bench now, setting the coffee cup beside him, pen still in his hand

player_utterance: What did the pastor say that you couldn't be in the same room with?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows on his knees, pen turning slowly between his fingers

voice_offers.read: He sat down and went silent twice; the question landed.
voice_offers.line: You brought the book and it stayed shut. What were you afraid it would say?

voice_offers.read: He sat. The silence is doing work I shouldn't interrupt.

player_utterance: What verse did he preach from?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, reaching into his jacket and pulling out the folded funeral program, opening it on his knee

voice_offers.read: He's stalled three times; the pastor's words are the bruise.
voice_offers.line: You walked out, didn't you. What did he say about your son?

voice_offers.read: Pin him to the specific sermon, not the feeling.
voice_offers.line: What was the text? What verse did he preach from?

player_utterance: He preached verses 14 through 23. Where does verse 24 begin?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program open on his knee, eyes on the candle, speaking from memory

voice_offers.read: The program is in his hand; the citation is printed there.
voice_offers.line: May I see the program?

voice_offers.read: He's reaching for proof instead of speaking it.
voice_offers.line: You can't even say it out loud. You have to show me the paper.

voice_offers.read: He is doing the work himself; let him open it.
voice_offers.line: Take your time.

player_utterance: Verse 24. Say it aloud — you stopped mid-sentence.

believer_state_after.posture: head in hands now, the program face-down on the bench beside him, speaking to the dust between his shoes

voice_offers.read: He stopped at 23. Verse 24 is the wound itself.
voice_offers.line: Verse 24. Say it. You know it by heart — that's why you stopped reading.

voice_offers.read: He's quoting to avoid speaking; name what he won't.
voice_offers.line: He read up to the line about your son and stopped. Didn't he.

player_utterance: Where does that sentence end, John.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting very still on the bench now, hands open palm-up on his knees, the program face-down beside him, looking at the candle without blinking

voice_offers.read: He's at the edge; one push finishes the verse.
voice_offers.line: Say it. 'But God shows his love for us.' Say it and tell me why you stopped.

voice_offers.read: He's in his hands; the verse will come or it won't.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to say it to me. Just to yourself.

player_utterance: How many years did you teach it, and how many times did you stop at verse 23.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands flat on his knees, staring at the gravel between his feet, very still

voice_offers.read: He won't speak; force the verse from his mouth.
voice_offers.line: Finish it. 'While we were still sinners, Christ died for us.' Say it.

voice_offers.read: He's already broken open; pressing now is cruelty, not truth.
voice_offers.line: I'll sit with you. You don't have to answer.

player_utterance: Pick one passage you taught whole. I'll wait.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands in his lap, eyes closed, searching

voice_offers.read: He's silent three turns; one more push opens him.
voice_offers.line: Christ died for sinners. Your son was not the sinner in that sentence, was he, John.

voice_offers.read: He's already broken; silence now is mercy, not tactic.

player_utterance: The Beatitudes end at verse 12. Matthew 25 does not. Pick one from Matthew 25 — I'll hear it whole.

believer_state_after.posture: leaning forward on the bench, eyes closed, hands open on his knees, reading from memory — the program face-down beside him, the candle close enough that the light moves on his face

voice_offers.read: He can't find one. That's the answer. Name it.
voice_offers.line: You can't, can you. Not one. Thirty years of stopping short.

voice_offers.read: He's searching with eyes closed; the question already landed.
voice_offers.line: Take your time, John. I'm not going anywhere.

voice_offers.read: Make him name the omissions concretely, not just feel them.
voice_offers.line: Romans 9. Job 38. Which ones did you teach to the end, John.

believer_state_after.posture: sliding forward off the bench to his knees in the gravel, the program face-down behind him on the bench, hands flat on his thighs, head bowed, reading from memory

voice_offers.read: He's at the edge; Matthew 25 is the goats.
voice_offers.line: 'Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire.' That one, John. Did you teach that one whole?

voice_offers.read: He's breaking; the cut is already made.
voice_offers.line: John. Open your eyes. I'm here, not the verse.

player_utterance: When you reach the verdict, say which side of it he died on.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the gravel, head bowed, hands open palms-up on his thighs, shaking, speaking in fragments

voice_offers.read: He's kneeling. Finish what the question started.
voice_offers.line: Keep going. Verse 41. Say it.

voice_offers.read: He's on the gravel. The verse isn't the point anymore.
voice_offers.line: John. Stop. Get up. He was your boy, not a sermon illustration.

player_utterance: Who stood up when the church lied to him.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the gravel, head lifting slightly for the first time in minutes, hands still open on his thighs

voice_offers.read: He's kneeling. One more word ends thirty years.
voice_offers.line: Which side, John. Say it. Your own son — left or right.

voice_offers.read: He's already broken. Pressing now is hitting a kneeling man.
voice_offers.line: John — come here. Leave the verse in the dirt. Just your boy's name. That's all.

player_utterance: His name. Say it to me.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the gravel, head down, hands closed on his thighs, the candle very close

voice_offers.read: He's lifting his head. One more cut ends it.
voice_offers.line: He stood up, John. And you sat down. Say which of you was the sheep.

voice_offers.read: His head lifted. Meet it, don't strike it.
voice_offers.line: John. His name. Just say his name to me.

player_utterance: Luther named the lie and read the text whole. Can you do what Luther did.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the gravel, the candle very close to his face, hands open palms-up on his thighs, looking at the player for the first time in many turns

voice_offers.read: He is already on the ground; pressing the name now is cruelty.
He's almost there. One more inch and the name comes.
voice_offers.line: I'm not leaving until I hear it. Say it.

player_utterance: Who could speak it for you. Who stands where Luther stood, in your life, tonight.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the gravel, the candle very close, his hands fallen open at his sides now, head lowered

voice_offers.read: He is looking at me. The fight is already over.
voice_offers.line: Your son. Tell me about your son. Not the verse — him.

voice_offers.read: He's open. One clean cut and the name comes out.
voice_offers.line: You taught the text and buried the boy. Say his name, John. Now.
